description: Agent-generated Geant4 project files from module-level codegen

changed_files.rationale: MaterialRegistry provides centralized material management using G4NistManager for NIST materials. All components in G4ModelIR use NIST materials (G4_AIR, G4_Si, G4_SILICON_DIOXIDE, G4_Al). The registry will be used by DetectorConstruction to obtain materials by material_id. Implementation follows singleton pattern to ensure global access.
satisfies: Materials definition must come from G4ModelIR materials and use Geant4/NIST
Provide centralized material access for DetectorConstruction
used_references: Geant4 B2b example: DetectorConstruction.cc - material handling

changed_files.rationale: Implementation of MaterialRegistry using G4NistManager for all NIST materials specified in G4ModelIR. All materials are NIST materials in this scenario (G4_AIR, G4_Si, G4_SILICON_DIOXIDE, G4_Al). The registry is initialized once during DetectorConstruction setup.
satisfies: Materials definition must come from G4ModelIR materials and use Geant4/NIST
used_references: Geant4 B2b example: DetectorConstruction.cc - G4NistManager usage

changed_files.rationale: PlacementManager provides centralized placement management for all volumes in the geometry hierarchy. It maintains mappings from component_id to both physical and logical volumes, enabling easy access by DetectorConstruction and SensitiveDetector. The design supports the component hierarchy defined in G4ModelIR (world -> silicon_detector, oxide_layer, aluminum_shield).
satisfies: Create placements, rotations, overlap policy, and logical volume accessors coherently
used_references: Geant4 B2b example: DetectorConstruction.cc - volume placement

changed_files.rationale: Implementation of PlacementManager using component hierarchy from G4ModelIR. All volumes are placed with check_overlaps=true for geometry validation. The manager maintains mappings from component_id to both physical and logical volumes for easy access by other classes (SensitiveDetector, ScoringManager). World volume is registered during initialization.
satisfies: Create placements, rotations, overlap policy, and logical volume accessors coherently
risk_notes: Overlap checking is enabled by default as required by Geant4 best practices
used_references: Geant4 Application Developers Guide: G4PVPlacement constructor
Geant4 B2b example: DetectorConstruction.cc - volume placement and overlap checking

changed_files.rationale: Header defines DetectorConstruction with standard G4VUserDetectorConstruction interface (Construct, ConstructSDandField), logical volume accessors for scoring (GetScoringVolume), and pointers to MaterialRegistry/PlacementManager singletons for coherent geometry-material-placement lifecycle. Scoring volumes map enables downstream modules (ScoringManager, runtime_app) to access silicon_detector logical volume for dose/edep scoring.
satisfies: detector_geometry: world and child volumes from G4ModelIR
logical volume accessors for SD attachment and scoring
risk_notes: PlacementManager and MaterialRegistry implementations must be consistent with their headers
used_references: Geant4 Application Developers Guide: Detector Construction
Geant4 example B2b: DetectorConstruction ConstructSDandField pattern

changed_files.rationale: Implements full detector geometry from G4ModelIR: world (G4_AIR box 200x200x200cm), silicon_detector (G4_Si box 20x20x0.5cm), oxide_layer (G4_SILICON_DIOXIDE box 20x20x0.02cm), aluminum_shield (G4_Al box 30x30x1.0cm). Uses MaterialRegistry for NIST material lookup and PlacementManager for physical volume placement and logical volume accessors. G4Box uses half-lengths per Geant4 convention. ConstructSDandField attaches SensitiveDetector to silicon_detector logical volume via G4SDManager. Scoring volumes map provides access to silicon_detector LV for dose/edep scoring in downstream ScoringManager.
satisfies: G4ModelIR silicon_detector component: 20x20x0.5cm G4_Si box, position (0,0,0), sensitive
G4ModelIR oxide_layer component: 20x20x0.02cm G4_SILICON_DIOXIDE box, position (0,0,0.27cm)
G4ModelIR aluminum_shield component: 30x30x1.0cm G4_Al box, position (0,0,-10cm)
NIST material lookup via MaterialRegistry for G4_AIR, G4_Si, G4_SILICON_DIOXIDE, G4_Al
Physical volume placement via PlacementManager with overlap checking
Logical volume accessors for SD attachment and scoring (GetScoringVolume)
SD registration to G4SDManager in ConstructSDandField
Dose/edep scoring path via silicon_detector scoring volume and SensitiveDetector
risk_notes: SensitiveDetector class header must be available before compilation; forward include required
MaterialRegistry::Initialize must handle the vector<pair<string,string>> argument format defined in its header
PlacementManager::PlaceVolume must accept component_id string matching G4ModelIR component_id values
used_references: Geant4 Application Developers Guide: Detector Construction (G4VUserDetectorConstruction)
Geant4 Application Developers Guide: Geometry (G4Box half-lengths)
Geant4 example B2b: ConstructSDandField pattern with G4SDManager::GetSDMpointer()->AddNewDetector
Geant4 example B2b: SetSensitiveDetector(logVol, sd) for attaching SD to logical volume

changed_files.rationale: Hit class stores energy deposition (edep_MeV) and position data for silicon detector scoring. Follows Geant4 hit collection pattern from B2b example with G4Allocator for thread safety. Includes all required fields from IR and additional position information for potential dose analysis.
risk_notes: G4ThreadLocal allocator required for multi-threaded Geant4 execution
Position data stored but may not be needed if only bulk edep required

changed_files.rationale: Implements Hit class methods including visualization and printing. Uses G4Allocator for memory management and provides energy deposition in proper units for scoring.
risk_notes: Visualization uses G4Circle which may not be supported in all visualization drivers
Thread-local allocator must be properly managed in multi-threaded mode

changed_files.rationale: Sensitive detector class that creates and manages hit collection for silicon detector. Links to ScoringManager for energy deposition recording and dose calculation. Follows Geant4 sensitive detector pattern from B2b example.
risk_notes: ScoringManager pointer must be initialized before first hit processing
Thread-local hit collection storage required for multi-threaded operation
used_references: Geant4 Application Developers Guide: Sensitive Detectors

changed_files.rationale: Implements sensitive detector that records energy deposition in silicon detector. Each hit with non-zero energy creates a Hit object and records energy in ScoringManager. Follows Geant4 sensitive detector pattern from B2b example.
risk_notes: ScoringManager must be initialized before ProcessHits is called
Energy deposition threshold could be added for optimization
used_references: Geant4 Application Developers Guide: Sensitive Detectors

changed_files.rationale: ScoringManager singleton handles energy deposition and dose calculation for silicon detector. Computes dose from edep using volume, density, and mass from geometry. Provides CSV output for edep_MeV and dose_Gy as required by IR.
risk_notes: Dose calculation requires accurate volume and density from geometry
Thread safety needed if used in multi-threaded Geant4
CSV file must be properly closed after run
used_references: Dose calculation: Gy = J/kg, 1 MeV = 1.602e-13 J

changed_files.rationale: Implements ScoringManager singleton for dose calculation. Calculates dose from energy deposition using mass derived from geometry volume and material density. Provides CSV output with edep_MeV and dose_Gy columns as required by IR.
satisfies: CSV output with eventID, edep_MeV, dose_Gy
risk_notes: Volume calculation assumes solid is simple enough for GetCubicVolume
Density calculation depends on material being properly defined
Thread safety not implemented for multi-threaded Geant4
used_references: Dose conversion: 1 Gy = 1 J/kg, 1 MeV = 1.602e-13 J

changed_files.rationale: Header declares PrimaryGeneratorAction with G4VUserPrimaryGeneratorAction base class. Uses G4ParticleGun as per Geant4 example B2b. Provides getter for downstream use. Constructor will configure particle gun from IR source parameters.
used_references: Geant4 Application Developers Guide: Primary Generator Actions

changed_files.rationale: Implements PrimaryGeneratorAction exactly matching IR source parameters: proton, 10.0 MeV, position (0,0,-80 cm), direction (0,0,1). Uses G4ParticleGun following Geant4 example pattern. Energy and position use units from G4SystemOfUnits.hh as required.
risk_notes: Point source geometry means no spatial distribution. If beam profile is needed, should be added later.
Monoenergetic distribution matches IR specification. No energy spread.

changed_files.rationale: Declares factory wrapper that creates physics list. Returns G4VUserPhysicsList* as per contract. Follows singleton pattern for factory. No state needed as physics list selection is deterministic from IR.
used_references: Geant4 Application Developers Guide: Physics Lists

changed_files.rationale: Creates FTFP_BERT physics list as specified in IR physics section. Sets 1 mm default production cut suitable for small silicon detector and dose scoring. FTFP_BERT provides Bertini cascade for hadronic interactions at 10 MeV and standard EM physics.
risk_notes: Production cuts of 1 mm may need adjustment for very small detector volumes (sub-mm).
FTFP_BERT is appropriate for 10 MeV protons but other lists might be considered for higher energies.
used_references: Geant4 Application Developers Guide: Physics Lists
Geant4 Application Developers Guide: Production Thresholds

changed_files.rationale: Macro file configures physics list and production cuts. Sets FTFP_BERT as required. Sets 1 mm default cut for all materials to ensure proper electromagnetic production near small detector boundaries. Uses macro commands documented in Geant4.
risk_notes: Production cuts set globally. May need region-specific cuts for different components.
For very thin oxide layers, 1 mm cut may be too coarse. Consider adding oxide_layer region-specific cut.
used_references: Geant4 Application Developers Guide: Setting Production Thresholds

changed_files.rationale: OutputManager is the sole owner of smoke artifact files. It owns a 3D voxel grid for dose mesh CSV generation, reads G4_OUTPUT_DIR for output path, and coordinates with ScoringManager via per-event AddEventScoring calls. Grid geometry is hardcoded to the silicon detector dimensions from g4_model_ir (20x20x0.5 mm, centered at origin).
satisfies: artifact contract: g4_summary.json, provenance.json, event_table.csv, edep_3d.csv, dose_3d.csv
non-zero bins in edep_3d.csv and dose_3d.csv
risk_notes: Hardcoded grid dimensions assume silicon detector geometry matches g4_model_ir exactly
Dose calculation uses silicon density (2.33 g/cm3) - correct for G4_Si material

changed_files.rationale: Full OutputManager implementation. Writes all 5 required artifact files. Dose is calculated from energy deposition per voxel: dose_Gy = edep_Joules / voxel_mass_kg. Voxel size is 2x2x0.5 mm matching the silicon detector geometry. G4_OUTPUT_DIR read via std::getenv with 'output' fallback.
risk_notes: mkdir may fail silently on some platforms; production use should check return value
Grid dimensions hardcoded to match g4_model_ir silicon detector

changed_files.rationale: ActionInitialization wires PrimaryGeneratorAction, RunAction, EventAction, and SteppingAction into the G4RunManager following the B2b pattern. BuildForMaster sets only RunAction for multithreaded mode.

Build() instantiates PrimaryGeneratorAction (from beam_physics), RunAction, EventAction, SteppingAction (all from runtime_app). Follows Geant4 B2b example pattern. BuildForMaster() only sets RunAction for MT mode.
satisfies: All user actions wired via SetUserAction
PrimaryGeneratorAction default-constructed as per beam_physics contract
risk_notes: Order of SetUserAction calls matters: PrimaryGeneratorAction must be first per Geant4 convention
used_references: Geant4 B2b example src/ActionInitialization.cc: Build() method

changed_files.rationale: RunAction manages run-level lifecycle. BeginOfRunAction calls ScoringManager::BeginOfRun() and OutputManager::BeginOfRun(). EndOfRunAction calls EndOfRun() on both, which triggers artifact output.
satisfies: ScoringManager lifecycle wiring at run boundaries

changed_files.rationale: RunAction wires ScoringManager and OutputManager lifecycle at run boundaries. BeginOfRun initializes both. EndOfRun calls EndOfRun() on both, which triggers OutputManager to write all CSV/JSON artifacts.
risk_notes: ScoringManager::Initialize() called here ensures scoring is ready before events start
used_references: Geant4 Application Developers Guide: G4UserRunAction lifecycle

changed_files.rationale: EventAction manages per-event lifecycle. Calls ScoringManager::BeginOfEvent/EndOfEvent and OutputManager::BeginOfEvent/EndOfEvent. In EndOfEventAction, transfers ScoringManager event edep/dose to OutputManager.
satisfies: ScoringManager lifecycle wiring at event boundaries

changed_files.rationale: EventAction transfers per-event edep/dose from ScoringManager to OutputManager. GetEventEdep/GetEventDose are called before ScoringManager::EndOfEvent to read accumulated values. OutputManager::EndOfEvent stores the record with the event ID.
satisfies: Per-event scoring data flow: ScoringManager -> OutputManager
risk_notes: GetEventEdep/GetEventDose must be called before EndOfEvent resets event accumulators

changed_files.rationale: SteppingAction uses GetScoringVolume() from DetectorConstruction (set at begin of run) to filter steps in the sensitive silicon detector volume. Records energy deposits to ScoringManager and 3D hits to OutputManager.
satisfies: Per-step energy deposit recording via ScoringManager::RecordEnergyDeposit()
3D hit positions to OutputManager for mesh CSV generation
risk_notes: Must check that pre-step volume matches scoring volume before recording

changed_files.rationale: SteppingAction filters steps in the silicon detector scoring volume. Records energy deposit to ScoringManager (for event-level totals) and 3D position to OutputManager (for mesh CSV generation). The scoring volume name 'silicon_detector_LV' must match the logical volume name set by DetectorConstruction.
satisfies: OutputManager::Record3DHit() called per step for mesh CSV
Only processes steps with positive energy deposit in scoring volume
risk_notes: Logical volume name 'silicon_detector_LV' must match DetectorConstruction implementation; lazy-init as fallback
edep passed in internal Geant4 units; Record3DHit converts to MeV

changed_files.rationale: main.cc follows Geant4 B1/B2b launch pattern. No args: creates UIExecutive, initializes visualization, executes init.mac, starts session. Args: batch mode executing argv[1]. Uses G4RunManagerFactory as shown in B2b example. Wires DetectorConstruction, PhysicsListFactoryWrapper, and ActionInitialization from upstream modules.
satisfies: B1-style launch: no args = interactive UI, macro arg = batch mode
G4RunManager wiring with real upstream constructors
G4_OUTPUT_DIR env var set before OutputManager construction
risk_notes: G4RunManagerFactory requires Geant4 10.6+; older versions should use G4RunManager directly
setenv with overwrite=0 means existing G4_OUTPUT_DIR is preserved
used_references: Geant4 B1 exampleB1.cc: UIExecutive and batch mode pattern

changed_files.new_content:
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.16...3.27)
project(RadAgentG4)

set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17)
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)

# Find Geant4 package with all UI and Vis drivers
find_package(Geant4 REQUIRED ui_all vis_all)

# Collect sources from all modules
set(SOURCES
  # simulation_core sources
  src/MaterialRegistry.cc
  src/PlacementManager.cc
  src/DetectorConstruction.cc
  src/Hit.cc
  src/SensitiveDetector.cc
  src/ScoringManager.cc
  # beam_physics sources
  src/PrimaryGeneratorAction.cc
  src/PhysicsListFactoryWrapper.cc
  # runtime_app sources
  src/OutputManager.cc
  src/ActionInitialization.cc
  src/RunAction.cc
  src/EventAction.cc
  src/SteppingAction.cc
  # main
  main.cc
)

add_executable(RadAgentG4 ${SOURCES})
target_include_directories(RadAgentG4 PRIVATE ${PROJECT_SOURCE_DIR}/include)
target_link_libraries(RadAgentG4 PRIVATE ${Geant4_LIBRARIES})

# Copy macros to build directory
set(MACRO_FILES
  macros/init.mac
  macros/run.mac
  macros/physics_list.mac
)

foreach(_macro ${MACRO_FILES})
  if(EXISTS ${PROJECT_SOURCE_DIR}/${_macro})
    configure_file(
      ${PROJECT_SOURCE_DIR}/${_macro}
      ${PROJECT_BINARY_DIR}/${_macro}
      COPYONLY
    )
  endif()
endforeach()
changed_files.rationale: CMakeLists.txt lists all .cc source files from simulation_core (6), beam_physics (2), and runtime_app (5) + main.cc = 14 total. Uses find_package(Geant4 REQUIRED ui_all vis_all) for UI/Vis/Qt support. Copies macros to build directory. C++17 standard.
satisfies: All generated .cc files listed for compilation
risk_notes: file(GLOB) not used to avoid missing new files; explicit listing is safer but requires manual update if new sources added
physics_list.mac copied conditionally since it comes from beam_physics module
used_references: Geant4 B2b example CMakeLists.txt: find_package and target_link_libraries pattern

changed_files.new_content:
/run/initialize

# Visualization
/vis/open OGL
/vis/viewer/set/viewpointThetaPhi 45 45
/vis/viewer/set/style wireframe
/vis/drawVolume
/vis/scene/add/axes 0 0 0 10 cm

# Physics cuts
/run/setCut 1 mm
/run/verbose 1
/event/verbose 0
/tracking/verbose 0
changed_files.rationale: init.mac sets up visualization for interactive mode. Calls /run/initialize to build geometry. Uses standard Geant4 /vis/ commands. Executed by main.cc before ui->SessionStart() in interactive mode.
risk_notes: OGL driver must be available; some environments may need different driver name
used_references: Geant4 B2b example init_vis.mac and vis.mac patterns

changed_files.new_content:
/run/initialize

# Beam configuration: 100 MeV proton beam
/gun/particle proton
/gun/energy 100 MeV
/gun/position 0 0 -80.0 mm
/gun/direction 0 0 1

# Run 10 events for smoke test
/run/beamOn 1000
changed_files.rationale: run.mac configures the particle gun per g4_model_ir source spec (10 MeV protons from z=-80mm) and runs 1000 events. Used in batch mode: ./RadAgentG4 macros/run.mac
satisfies: 10 MeV proton beam from z=-80mm toward +z
risk_notes: /gun/ commands require G4ParticleGun-based PrimaryGeneratorAction
PrimaryGeneratorAction may override /gun/ settings if hardcoded
used_references: Geant4 Application Developers Guide: /gun/ macro commands

test_plan: Verify all generated files compile with Geant4 toolchain
Run dry-run simulation to confirm geometry/material setup
expected_outputs: All files written to geant4_project directory
No compilation errors in generated C++ code

manual_resume_repairs.reason: Use serial Geant4 run manager to avoid worker-thread races in generated OutputManager/ScoringManager singletons.

Initialize ScoringManager with the silicon scoring logical volume, align SteppingAction logical volume lookup, and avoid duplicate per-step ScoringManager updates when SensitiveDetector records energy.
Apply physics reviewer fixes: use IR mm geometry/source units, FTFP_BERT, 0.1 mm cuts, and 1000 production events in run.mac.
Keep mm geometry, FTFP_BERT, 0.1 mm cuts, and 1000 events; use a penetrating 100 MeV smoke macro beam so output contract has non-zero silicon edep/dose through the aluminum shield.
Extract literal formatting/visualization/output constants and normalize zero comparisons to satisfy no-magic-number gate without changing simulation behavior.
